hero.description: Full Stack Developer specialized in Angular and NestJS. I build commercial systems with TypeScript, PostgreSQL and Docker. Real experience in production for real users. Based in Sucre, Bolivia.

about.title: About Me
bio: I'm Manuel Adolfo Soto, a Full Stack developer and Computer Engineering student (about to defend my thesis) at USFX, Sucre, Bolivia.

I completed my full-time convention internship at Afer Bolivia (Mar 2026 - Jun 2026), building an e-commerce system for home appliances. I worked on role-based authentication, CRUDs, inventory, orders, admin panel and reports, collaborating with a multidisciplinary team of design, systems, commercial and economics.
I'm currently continuing my English studies (A1 level) and looking for remote work opportunities to keep growing professionally.

afer.name: Afer Bolivia
afer.description: Home appliance e-commerce with a complete online store system. Includes role-based authentication (admin/user/client), product and inventory CRUD, order management, shopping cart, admin panel with reports and sales module. Built for real users, nearing deployment. Replaces and improves a previous existing system.
afer.problem: The legacy system didn't scale, lacked role-based authentication and had simultaneous user limits, affecting daily business operations.
afer.result: Modern Angular + NestJS system replacing the old one, supporting multiple roles (admin, user, client), cart, inventory and real-time reports. Nearing production deployment.

farmacia.description: Pharmacy management system with inventory control, medication expiry tracking, sales, reports and administration module. University project with layered architecture.

blog.title: Technical Blog

post.title: JWT Authentication with roles in NestJS
post.intro: At Afer Bolivia I implemented a role-based authentication system (admin, user, client) using JWT in NestJS. Each role has specific permissions over resources like products, orders and reports.

sections.heading: Role structure
sections.body: I defined a role enum (admin, user, client) and a custom guard that checks the authenticated user's role before allowing access to each endpoint. This allows reusing the same logic across all controllers.

sections.heading: Token generation
sections.body: On login, the server validates credentials and generates a JWT with the user's ID and role. The token is sent to the frontend (Angular) which stores it and attaches it to every request.

sections.heading: Route protection
sections.body: I used NestJS guards with custom decorators to protect routes by role. For example, only admin can access the reports panel, while client can view their order history.

post.conclusion: This system taught me the importance of designing security from the start and how NestJS makes it easy with clean, reusable guards and decorators.
